McLane requires the capability to change zones for pick locations throughout the day depending on volume need. During task creation, WM assigns a picker number (i.e. Zone Number) to tasks (TASK_DTL.PIKR_NBR) specifying the associated pick zones needed for zone picking. MCLN supervisors have the capability to reconfigure location zones as needed post task creation/ wave complete, depending on the volume for the day. If zones are updated post wave, base WM does not update existing tasks and allocation records with the updates made to pick zones. McLane requires the ability to direct existing tasks to different pick zones and maintain allocation records. To fulfil this requirement, a custom Manage Zone UI is developed in WMOS to account for zones based on volume for the day. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) Y Custom UI – Manage Zone UI, Manage Zone – Rezone UI , Manage Zone – Zone Rebalance UI MHE N RF N Reporting N Other N McLane utilizes this Manage Zone UI to update the location grouping attribute specifying the zone of the location /bay (s). WMOS is enhanced to update picker numbers (TASK_DTL.PIKR_NBR and AID.PIKR_NBR ) and LPN.FIRST_ZONE on existing tasks, in released or locked/disabled status, upon updating the location group attribute (LOCN_GRP.GRP_ATTR) in this Manage Zone UI as per EX103. Assumptions EX33 Zone Picking must be enabled. Transaction Parameter 5-5 is set to ‘Defines if static is used’ for Pack Pick Cart transactions . Wave parameter for zone picking is set to ‘Static’. Only INT-50 tasks are supported . EX103 Updates only supports Active locations . EX103 updates occur only for tasks in status locked/disabled or released , not tasks currently in progress. Custom Zone must be a numerical value (1-9999) Bays can only have 1 Zone a ttribute and cannot be shared across zones . Location Ranges for zone picking are set up continuously by Container Type within a floor (Work group) , not breaking LOCN _PIC _SEQ. EX.) scenarios where LOCN 1 and 3 are part of zone 1 but LOCN 2 is no t is considered invalid. Users can customize what columns will be displayed in the Tasks UI Only s ingle value c olumns are sortable and filterable within Screen 1 of the Manage Zones UI Locations are eligible to be rezoned while there are no waves running Locations with differing container types can not belong to the same set of zone s Users actively picking are eligible to be directed to different zones after locations are rezoned . Allocations/ oLPNs / Tasks must exist Multiple selector s in one zone range is allowed Values displayed in each UI will reflect the values held in the database at the moment they were opened. Changes made after the UI was opened will not be reflected unless the UI is refreshed. For this reason, the number of tasks/ oLPNs after saving may be different than what is displayed initially WMOS does not create more zones than there are available bays ,or directly affect the number of units required for each location. When rebalancing, WMOS gets as close to equivalent as is possible within these constraints. Multiple work areas in a single zone are allowed EX103 updates only support locations included in custom code C- REZ Outbound rules utilized for EX103 contain at least one or more work areas Work areas are not shared across outbound rules utilized by EX103 Work areas not defined in outbound rules utilized by EX103 are not affected or displayed in custom Manage Zones UI Code Id’s configured in custom code C-REZ must be a valid container type Dependencies Code Dependencies EX33 CR21 - RF Picking for Active Zone Picking Enhancement Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement Platform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Process Flow Diagram User runs waves to generate zone picking tasks User navigates to custom EX103 Manage Zones UI IF user has WMAMZ permission then custom UI is populated. ELSE No custom UI is populated Zone information is populated on the screen. User updates zone based on user actions: Expand to the Left Expand to the Right Zone Rebalance User applies changes to preview before selecting submit. Defining Zone Location Assignments EX103 requires the below column name to be updated to a numerical value ranging from 1-99 9 9 for zone picking to function within EX33. The below column name ( LOCN_GRP.GRP_ATTR ) is utilized to assign a Zone Picking Location a dedicated Zone Number Attribute . A Zone Picking Locations can only have 1 Zone Number attribute and cannot be shared across zones. Once a wave is ran and the Wave parameter for zone picking is set to ‘Static’ , Zone Picking Locations and its associated task’s TASK_DTL.PIKR_NBR and allocation AID.PIKR_NBR will get populated with the location zone number (LOCN_GRP.GRP_ATTR). Table Name Column Name Data Type Description Nullable? LOCN_GRP GRP_ATTR Varchar( 20) Contains Zone Picking Number Y EX103 also requires the below custom system code to be configured. The system code C-REZ-XXX is set up where each code id is a valid container type and its parameter value is the outbound rule associated with th e container type. WM looks at the work area ( s ) ( RULE_SEL_DTL.COLM_NAME = 'WORK_AREA' ) within the outbound rule ( RULE_HDR.RULE_NAME ) and utilizes the location /bay (s) associated with the work area ( s ) . First, the extension specific sys code C- REZ parameter values must be configured. This is controlled through the custom system code C -REZ -XXX where XXX represents the code id of the desired container type . Th e parameter within each code id is configured as an alphanumeric edit box. If the edit box is configured with a valid Outbound Rule , then WMOS continues with the custom processing below. Otherwise, no custom EX 103 updates occur. Enablement of the Mange Zones UI is controlled through the custom WMA MZ permission . . Screen 1 - Manage Zones UI WM will then utilize the populated Zone Picking Locations based on LOCN_GRP.GRP_ATTR and locations found in sys code C- REZ allow for a customized WM UI- Manage Zones UI to appear . Screen 1 - A ssociate s can filter the waves on this UI by wave, floor, work group, container type and/or zone number. The associate then views the information available on the screen and select s Rezone under the actions column to proceed to screen 2 - Rezone UI to reconfigure the zone . This validation is performed on the location range being consumed on the left . WMOS does not allow for expand ing to the left for the selected zone range with the smallest, lowest value zone number for each respected floor . Updates: Perform the following screen changes : WM will not commit updates until the user hits the “Save” Button. {Zone Range} = Increase selected zone range’s ending bay by +1, increase next zone range’s (selected zone range’s Zone number + 1) beginning bay by + 1. { Bay } = Increase selected zone range’s bay count by +1, Decrease next zone range’s (selected zone range’s Zone number - 1) bay count by -1. {Lines} = Re-evaluate the total count of Order Lines based on the updated zone range {Units} = Re-evaluate the total count of Units based on the updated zone range {Container Start} = Re-evaluate the total count of oLPNs having its first pick beginning at this zone range based on the updated zone range {Induction with Picks} = Re-evaluate the number of oLPNs that are being currently being worked on at that point in time based on the updated zone range Continuation of Scenario example: User selects Zone Numbers 4 to expand to the right . Updates: Perform the following updates: WM commits the screen changes made when user invoked either “Expand to the Left’, “Expand to the Right” and commit DB updates to reconfigured zone locations. IF a Zone Picking Location’s zone range attribute (Zone Number ) is update d , then WM update s the tas k associated to the updated zone picking location ’s TASK_HDR .BEGIN_ZONE, TASK_DTL.PIKR_NBR , AID.PIKR_NBR , and LPN.FIRST_ZONE to align with the new zone range attribute (Zone Number) . Th ese update s occur for tasks/ oLPNs in less than ‘ IN PACKING ’ status. If there are multiple zones associated with a single task, then TASK_HDR.BEGIN_ZONE should be populated with the lowest numerical zone associated with it. Screen 3 – Zone Rebalance UI The Manage Zones – Zone Rebalance UI displays if the Zone Rebalance button is clicked on the Manage Zone – Rezone UI. This UI allows the user to re balance zones based on the number of zones available. Once users enter a valid entry for Nbr of Zones, WM return the user to screen 2 with the updated logic values; however, WM will not commit updates until the user hits the “Save” Button. WM will display the Rezone Rebalance updated values through the Screen UI changes until the user hits save – allowing for Supervisors to Expand to the Left, Expand to the Right as needed . Once the Save button is invoked, WM will confirm the user screen changes to Screen 2 – Rezone UI and perform confirmation updates as needed. Display This UI is used to balances zones based on the number of zones the user enters . Note that actual updated values are dependent on available open tasks per zone. If a user enters more zones than can physically exist, WMOS will instead create the maximum number of zones possible (with the maximum being as many zones as there are bays .) The same logic holds true for the number of units. If one location has a lar ge number of required units , EX103 does not introduce any logic to affect this and will create as close to an equal number of units per zone as is possible. # Literal Source Display Length (R/L justified) 1 ‘ Nbr of Zones : ’ User Entry 0 2 A (L) User Entry # Field Description Display Length (R/L justified) 1 ‘ Nbr of Zones :’ Determines the number of Zone s the current Floor (Work Group) /Container type will be rebalance d with based on number of units. 2N (R) User Actions Submit Allows for multiple selection : N/A Validations: Validate that user entered is a valid numeric field from 1-99 . If user entered value is 0 , display error CUST 1030 2 “ Value cannot be zero. Please enter value greater than 0.” Updates : If validation passes, WM performs the following updates /logic: WM first stores t he user entered value for Nbr of Zones in memory . This value will be used to determine how many locations/bays will be needed within each unique zone range to allow for the “ zone rebalance ” to be split evenly (or as evenly as possible) across zone ranges based on the number of units left to be picked. WM then reevaluates all available zone-picking locations with open picks . WM determines the overall count of the available zone-picking locations based on work group / container type . Sample SQL For Display: (This is an example to validate data, needs to be modified/tuned for development)   SELECT DISTINCT ( WA.WORK _GRP) AS WORK_GROUP, COUNT( DISTINCT L.TC_LPN_ID) AS TOTAL_LPNs, TD.QTY_ALLOC AS REMAINING_QTY FROM WORK_AREA_MASTER WA, LPN L, TASK_DTL TD, SHIPMENT S WHERE TD.CURR _WORK_GRP = WA.WORK _GRP AND L.TC_LPN_ID = TD.CARTON _NBR AND TD.CURR _WORK_AREA=WA.WORK_AREA AND L.TC_SHIPMENT_ID = S.TC_SHIPMENT_ID AND S.TC_SHIPMENT_ID = ' 'RECORD FOR EVERY SHIPMENT” ( SHIPMENT FOUND) --AND TD.CURR _WORK_GRP = ' ' RECORD FOR EVERY WORK GROUP (WORK GROUP FOUND) --AND TD.CURR _WORK_AREA = ' ' RECORD FOR EVERY WORK AREA (WORK AREA FOUND) AND L.LPN_STATUS < 20 GROUP BY WA.WORK _GRP ORDER BY WA.WORK _GRP ASC; WM then determines the total count of units per location/bay dedicated for zone picking still needing to be picked on Tasks/ oLPNs not in completed/packed status within a specific wave /shipment . Sample SQL For Display: (This is an example to validate data, needs to be modified/tuned for development) SELECT DISTINCT (LH.DSP_LOCN) AS LOCATION, TD.QTY_ ALLOC AS TOTAL_REMAINING_UNITS FROM WORK_AREA_MASTER WA, LPN L, TASK_DTL TD, SHIPMENT S, LOCN_HDR LH WHERE TD.CURR _WORK_GRP = WA.WORK _GRP AND L.TC_LPN_ID = TD.CARTON _NBR AND TD.CURR _WORK_AREA=WA.WORK_AREA AND L.TC_SHIPMENT_ID = S.TC_SHIPMENT_ID AND LH.LOCN _ID = L.PICK _SUB_LOCN_ID AND L.WAVE _NBR = ' ' WAVE NUMBER” AND S.TC_SHIPMENT_ID = ' 'RECORD FOR EVERY SHIPMENT” ( SHIPMENT FOUND) AND TD.CURR _WORK_GRP = ' ' RECORD FOR EVERY WORK GROUP (WORK GROUP FOUND) AND TD.CURR _WORK_AREA = ' ' RECORD FOR EVERY WORK AREA (WORK AREA FOUND) AND L.LPN_STATUS < 20 GROUP BY LH.DSP_LOCN ORDER BY LH.DSP_LOCN ASC; WM then uses the user entered value for Nbr of Zones to evenly split (or as evenly as possible ) the location/ bays up into zones ranges equal to the Nbr of Zones entry and each location/bay’s total number of units left to be picked . WM selects the appropriate number of locations , which fulfill the new evenly split quantity, in ascending PICK_LOCN_SEQ sequence . After calculating, WM return the user to screen 2 with the updated logic values. This will serve as a “Preview” Screen such that supervisors can validate zones and expand zones to the Left/Right as needed. NOTE: If only part ial remaining QTY of a location is needed to fulfill the unique zone range , then the entirety of the location and its remaining QTY is included in the first zone range . McLane requires the capability to change zones for pick locations throughout the day depending on volume need. During task creation, WM assigns a picker number (i.e. Zone Number) to tasks (TASK_DTL.PIKR_NBR) specifying the associated pick zones needed for zone picking. MCLN supervisors have the capability to reconfigure location zones as needed post task creation/ wave complete, depending on the volume for the day. If zones are updated post wave, base WM does not update existing tasks and allocation records with the updates made to pick zones. McLane requires the ability to direct existing tasks to different pick zones and maintain allocation records. To fulfil this requirement, a custom Manage Zone UI is developed in WMOS to account for zones based on volume for the day.

Screen 3 – Zone Rebalance UI The Manage Zones – Zone Rebalance UI displays if the Zone Rebalance button is clicked on the Manage Zone – Rezone UI. This UI allows the user to re balance zones based on the number of zones available. Once users enter a valid entry for Nbr of Zones, WM return the user to screen 2 with the updated logic values; however, WM will not commit updates until the user hits the “Save” Button. WM will display the Rezone Rebalance updated values through the Screen UI changes until the user hits save – allowing for Supervisors to Expand to the Left, Expand to the Right as needed . Once the Save button is invoked, WM will confirm the user screen changes to Screen 2 – Rezone UI and perform confirmation updates as needed. Display This UI is used to balances zones based on the number of zones the user enters . Note that actual updated values are dependent on available open tasks per zone. If a user enters more zones than can physically exist, WMOS will instead create the maximum number of zones possible (with the maximum being as many zones as there are bays .) The same logic holds true for the number of units. If one location has a lar ge number of required units , EX103 does not introduce any logic to affect this and will create as close to an equal number of units per zone as is possible. # Literal Source Display Length (R/L justified) 1 ‘ Nbr of Zones : ’ User Entry 0 2 A (L) User Entry # Field Description Display Length (R/L justified) 1 ‘ Nbr of Zones :’ Determines the number of Zone s the current Floor (Work Group) /Container type will be rebalance d with based on number of units. 2N (R) User Actions Submit Allows for multiple selection : N/A Validations: Validate that user entered is a valid numeric field from 1-99 .
